title: cistus polymorphus subsp. villosus
intro: Cistus polymorphus subsp. villosus, also known as hairy rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a woody shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its hairy leaves and white, pink, or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. Cistus polymorphus subsp. villosus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
